title: Automatically installing recommended packages is security-sensitive
description: Automatically installing recommended packages is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotdockerfileInstalling recommended packages automatically can lead to vulnerabilities in the Docker image.Potentially unnecessary packages are installed via a known Debian package manager. These packages will increase the attack surface of the created container as they might contain unidentified vulnerabilities or malicious code. Those packages could be used as part of a broader supply chain attack.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

# Sensitive
RUN apt install -y build-essential

# Sensitive
RUN apt-get install -y build-essential

# Sensitive
RUN aptitude install -y build-essential
compliant:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

RUN apt --no-install-recommends install -y build-essential

RUN apt-get --no-install-recommends install -y build-essential

RUN aptitude --without-recommends install -y build-essential

checks.message: Automatically installing recommended packages is security-sensitive. Recommended packages may contain vulnerabilities or unnecessary code that increases the attack surface. Use --no-install-recommends flag for apt/apt-get or --without-recommends for aptitude to avoid installing unnecessary packages.
